platform_decisions.content: Issue triage automation triggers on issue.opened and issue_comment.created (human-only, not bot comments). Separation of human signal from bot noise. Triage should respond to human interactions but avoid cascading bot loops. Filters: github.event.comment.user.type != 'Bot'

Concurrent triage jobs use issue-level locking with cancel-in-progress=true. Prevent duplicate triage runs on same issue. Latest triage request wins. Concurrency group: issue-triage-${{ github.event.issue.number }}

Claude Code triage permits all non-write users via allowed_non_write_users: '*'. Enable community issue triage without requiring write access. Read-only GitHub token acceptable for triage classification tasks.

Issue lifecycle comments posted by scheduled label event, not by triage step itself. Decouple triage labeling logic from lifecycle messaging. Label application triggers separate workflow (issue-lifecycle-comment.yml) which calls bun script to post context-aware messages.

Non-write-users-check prevents PR merges that modify allowed_non_write_users without human review. Security gate for permission escalation. Detects changes to .github/*.yml that add allowed_non_write_users param, blocks with comment requesting AppSec review.

Duplicate detection idempotence via comment marker (<!-- non-write-users-check -->). Avoid re-commenting on same PR. Workflow checks existing comments for marker before posting. Prevents spam on iterative PRs.

Issue deduplication workflow triggers on issue.opened OR workflow_dispatch (manual backfill). Auto-detect new duplicates immediately, allow manual backfill over issue history (90 days default). Permits both automated and scheduled catch-up runs.
Statsig event logging in dedup workflow runs even if dedupe fails (if: always()). Observability over success. Track all dedup attempts (success/failure) for analytics. Decoupled from business logic.
Stale issue locking scheduled at 14:00 UTC daily (8am Pacific during DST). Time-based maintenance window outside peak US hours. 7-day inactivity threshold before lock. Batch locking reduces API load.

Issue lock adds comment before locking, skips PRs, respects existing locks. Give context to users why issue locked. Separate issues from PRs (different lifecycle). Don't re-lock already-locked issues.

Auto-close duplicates runs on cron schedule (09:00 UTC daily) via bun script, not API directly. Batch processing reduces API load. Bun script provides flexibility for complex duplicate detection logic (likely ML-based).

Autoclose label removal triggered on issue_comment.created (human comments only, not bot). Human activity removes autoclose label, letting issues stay open if user responds. Filters out github-actions[bot] comments to avoid noise.

Issue lifecycle timeouts enforced twice daily (10:00 and 22:00 UTC). Global schedules to ensure even coverage across timezones. 22:00 UTC catches APAC morning hours. Concurrency group: daily-issue-sweep prevents overlaps.

Issue opened dispatch sends to external repo via repository dispatch event. Decouple issue tracking from downstream systems. Target repo stored in secret (ISSUE_OPENED_DISPATCH_TARGET_REPO). Graceful exit on dispatch failure (|| exit 0).

Event logging to Statsig escapes shell variables via environment vars, not template injection. Injection attack prevention. All user-controlled values (title, author, etc.) passed via env vars. Sed escapes quotes in title before curl.

@claude mention in issue/PR triggers Claude Code action (must contain @claude in body/title/comment/review). Opt-in activation. Prevents Claude from running on every issue. Covers issue body, title, comment, and PR review mentions.

Claude Code action permissions: contents:read, pull-requests:read, issues:read, id-token:write. Minimal privileges. Read-only for repo content. id-token:write allows OIDC federation for API auth (no explicit secrets passed).

Blank issues disabled (blank_issues_enabled: false) with contact_links to Discord/docs. Force structured reporting. Direct lost users to support channels (Discord, docs, troubleshooting). Reduces low-signal issues.

Bug report preflight checklist enforces version check, existing issue search, and single-bug-per-issue rule. Reduce duplicate reports and out-of-date reports. Force users to confirm latest version before reporting. Cognitive barrier to multi-bug reports.

Bug report captures regression detection (yes/no/unknown) and last-working-version. Triage hint. Regressions often easy to fix if bisect is clear. Unknown/don't-know answers still valuable for triage prioritization.

Bug report requires platform, OS, terminal/shell, and Claude model selection. Environment specificity. Bugs often tied to platform/OS/terminal combo. Model selection helps identify model-specific regression.

Feature request preflight enforces single-feature-per-issue and duplicate search. Avoid feature fragmentation. One feature per issue allows independent voting/prioritization. Duplicate search prevents bikeshedding.

Feature request separates problem statement from proposed solution (avoid leading the solution). Problem-first mindset. Capture user pain point before solution design. Allows triage team to suggest alternative solutions.

Feature request requires priority (critical/high/medium/low) and feature category selection. Prioritization metadata. Priority + category allows bulk triage and roadmap planning. User-declared priority guides team focus.

Model behavior template captures behavior type (file modification, permission, assumptions) to isolate safety issues. Safety triage. Separate unexpected-but-harmless behavior from genuine safety violations (file access, permission bypass). Enables fast path for safety issues.

Model behavior template requires reproduction steps and capture of exact prompt + actual behavior + expected behavior. Reproducibility for model behavior debugging. Exact prompt crucial for model tracing. Step-by-step allows triage team to reproduce in isolation.

Model behavior template captures permission mode (Accept Edits on/off/unknown) and reproducibility (always/sometimes/once). Context for root cause. Permission mode determines if changes were auto-approved or human-approved. Reproducibility guides investigation strategy (regression vs edge case).

Documentation template categorizes issues (missing/unclear/incorrect/typo/broken links) to separate content gaps from quality issues. Clear separation of concerns. Missing docs need authoring. Unclear/incorrect docs need editing. Typos/links need quick fixes. Enables parallel triage tracks.

Documentation template requires impact assessment (high/medium/low) to prioritize high-impact gaps. ROI-driven docs work. High-impact issues (blocks usage) prioritized over nice-to-have improvements. Allows triage to cut scope in documentation sprints.

Backfill duplicate comments supports dry-run mode via workflow_dispatch input. Safe testing of bulk operations. Dry-run logs what would be done without side effects. Allows human verification before production run.

Backfill duplicate comments defaults to 90 days lookback, customizable via workflow_dispatch. Balance coverage with scope. 90 days captures most active issues without overwhelming queue. Customization allows focused backfills (e.g., last 30 days).
